title: Dog Fly Strike on Ear Tips: Symptoms, Treatment, and Prevention
meta_desc: Learn about fly strike on your dog's ears, why it is a low-urgency condition, and how photos help your vet provide the best triage care.
content_html:
What is Fly Strike on Dog Ears?

Fly strike, technically known as fly strike dermatitis, occurs when biting flies—most commonly stable flies—repeatedly attack the thin skin on the tips of a dog's ears. These flies bite the skin to feed on blood, causing small, painful sores that often crust over with dark, bloody scabs. This condition is frequently seen in dogs that spend significant time outdoors during warmer months.

Is This an Emergency?

The urgency level for fly strike is Low. While the condition is uncomfortable and can cause your dog significant irritation, it is not a life-threatening emergency. However, if the sores are left untreated, they can attract more flies, lead to secondary bacterial infections, or cause permanent scarring and thickening of the ear margins. You should schedule a routine veterinary appointment to receive proper medicated ointments and repellent recommendations.

How a Photo Helps Triage

Providing a clear, well-lit photo of your dog's ear tips is an essential part of the triage process. A visual assessment allows a veterinary professional to confirm if the lesions are consistent with fly bites or if they might be signs of other conditions, such as ear margin vasculitis or sarcoptic mange. Close-up images help us determine the severity of the inflammation and provide immediate home-care advice to protect the ears until you can see a vet.

faqs.question: Can I use human fly repellent on my dog?
faqs.answer: No, many human insect repellents contain DEET, which is toxic to dogs if licked or absorbed. Always use products specifically labeled as pet-safe.

faqs.question: How can I protect my dog's ears right now?
faqs.answer: The most effective immediate step is to keep your dog indoors during the day when flies are most active. You can also gently clean the area with mild soap and water if your dog allows it.

faqs.question: Will the hair on the ear tips grow back?
faqs.answer: Yes, in most cases the hair will grow back once the biting stops and the skin has had time to fully heal, though chronic cases may result in slight thickening of the skin.
